onboarding.languagePageSubtitle: Choose the language Maumau should use during onboarding and chat replies. Internal prompts and skills stay in English.
onboarding.languagePageFootnote: You can change this during onboarding by going back to this step.
onboarding.nextStepsMeaningTitle: Here’s what the next steps mean
onboarding.setupLegend: Required steps are marked Required. Optional steps can be done later. Needs prep means you may need another app, account, or device ready for that step.
onboarding.securityNoticeBody:
Maumau can do real things on your Mac if you turn them on, like run commands, read or change files, and take screenshots.

Only continue if that makes sense to you and you trust the AI and tools you connect.
onboarding.connectionIntro: Gateway means Maumau's home. Most people choose This Mac, which means this computer keeps the tools and does the work here.
onboarding.checkingHelperToolsLabel: Checking the helper tools this Mac needs…
onboarding.runtimeAlreadyAvailableHint: If Node 22+ is already here, Maumau can keep going without reinstalling anything.
onboarding.localSetupRunningHint: Maumau is getting this Mac ready before the next step.
onboarding.localSetupReadyHint: This Mac is ready. Continue to the brain setup.
onboarding.permissionsIntro: These are the main Mac permissions Maumau uses when it helps with apps, windows, or screenshots. Turn on only the ones you want.
onboarding.optionalLaterBody: Voice Wake, camera, and location stay out of the way here. If you want those later, you can turn them on in Settings.
onboarding.wizardIntro: Brain means the AI service Maumau uses for thinking and writing. Choose it once, sign in once, and Maumau will remember your default choice.
onboarding.wizardCompleteTitle: Wizard complete. Continue to the next step.
onboarding.wizardSkippedTitle: Brain setup skipped for now. You can finish it later in Settings.
onboarding.channelsIntro: Channel means the app where people text Maumau. Think of it like giving Maumau a phone line or inbox. Pick one now, and you can add more later.
onboarding.loadingChatAppsTitle: Loading chat apps from the Gateway…
onboarding.loadingChatAppsHint: If this stays empty, make sure the Gateway is running, then hit Refresh.
onboarding.privateAccessIntro: This gives Maumau's home a private driveway. It lets your phone, laptop, or browser reach Maumau privately without putting Maumau on the public internet.
onboarding.privateAccessThisMacSubtitle: Use Install on this Mac below. Maumau downloads the official Tailscale installer here, macOS asks for your administrator password, then you sign in here.
onboarding.privateAccessOtherDevicesSubtitle: When you want to open Maumau from your phone or another laptop, install Tailscale on that device later and sign in to the same private network there.
onboarding.privateAccessDefaultPrivacySubtitle: Private mode keeps Maumau off the public internet. Only devices you add to the same private Tailscale network can open the private link.
onboarding.privateAccessSafetySubtitle: In private mode, Maumau accepts only Tailscale's verified private-network identity for the dashboard and live connection. If you want an extra lock, require a Maumau password too.
onboarding.privateAccessLaterSubtitle: The same guide stays in Settings → General, so you can run the install here later, sign in later, or add password protection later if you skip this for now.
onboarding.conversationAutomationIntro: Choose the simple Vapi path or keep the advanced self-hosted path for real phone calls. Both options stay inside the built-in voice-call plugin.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTelephonySubtitle: Maumau will only save voice-call settings when every required provider key and callback route is ready.
onboarding.conversationAutomationChecklistSubtitle: Simple with Vapi: Vapi API key, one assistant, one imported Twilio number, and Private Access ready. Advanced self-hosted: phone provider credentials, a public callback URL, realtime speech-to-text, and ElevenLabs.
onboarding.conversationAutomationModeSubtitle: Simple with Vapi is the quick outbound calling path. Advanced self-hosted keeps the current direct-provider flow.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiSubtitle: Use Vapi for the live voice pipeline, import a Twilio number there, and let Maumau handle the conversation brain and memory.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiAPIKeySubtitle: Paste your Vapi private API key, then connect to load assistants and phone numbers from your Vapi account.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiAssistantSubti: Choose the Vapi assistant Maumau should use as the base for outbound calls.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiAssistantEmpty: Connect to Vapi first, then choose one assistant here.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiPhoneNumberSub: Choose the imported Twilio number Vapi should call from.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiPhoneNumberEmp: Connect to Vapi after importing a Twilio number, then choose that number here.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiPreferredLangu: Default spoken replies to the language you want callers to hear first.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiBridgeModeSubt: Auto bridge publishes a public callback for Vapi on a separate Tailscale Funnel port. Manual public URL lets you point Vapi at another public bridge.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiAutoBridgeWait: Auto bridge uses Tailscale Funnel on public port 8443. Finish Private Access first so Maumau can publish that bridge URL.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiBridgeWaitingS: Finish Private Access first so Maumau has a public bridge URL for Vapi.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiManualBridgeSu: Paste a public HTTPS URL if you want Vapi to call Maumau through another bridge instead of the auto-managed Tailscale path.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiOutboundOnlySu: This simple path is for outbound calls first. Inbound routing and manual live call controls stay in Advanced self-hosted.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiIndonesiaNotic: Indonesia numbers depend on Twilio inventory and regulation. If +62 is not available right now, buy or port in Twilio first, then import that number into Vapi.
onboarding.conversationAutomationPhoneProviderSubti: Choose the built-in phone provider Maumau should use for live calls, then add that provider's number and credentials below.
onboarding.conversationAutomationPhoneNumberSubtitl: Paste the E.164 number Maumau should call from, like +628123456789.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTwilioSectionSubti: Use the Twilio Console to get a voice-capable number and copy the account credentials Maumau needs for calls.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTwilioAccountSIDSu: Copy the Account SID from your Twilio Console project.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTwilioAuthTokenSub: Reveal or create the Auth Token in Twilio Console, then paste it here.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTelnyxSectionSubti: Use Telnyx Mission Control to get a voice-capable number, create an API key, and open the Call Control connection or application you want Maumau to use.
onboarding.conversationAutomationPlivoSectionSubtit: Use the Plivo Console to buy or assign a voice-capable number, then copy the auth credentials Maumau needs.
onboarding.conversationAutomationPlivoAuthIDSubtitl: Copy the Auth ID from your Plivo Console account.
onboarding.conversationAutomationPlivoAuthTokenSubt: Copy the Auth Token from your Plivo Console account.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTelnyxAPIKeySubtit: Create a Telnyx API v2 key and paste it here.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTelnyxConnectionID: Paste the connection or application ID from the Telnyx Call Control setup.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTelnyxPublicKeySub: Maumau uses this to verify signed Telnyx webhooks.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTelnyxPublicKeyPla: Paste the public key from Telnyx
onboarding.conversationAutomationWebhookSubtitle: Your chosen phone provider must be able to reach Maumau over HTTPS during live calls. Pick the public route Maumau should use.
onboarding.conversationAutomationWebhookManualLabel: I already have a public webhook URL
onboarding.conversationAutomationWebhookPublicURLSu: Paste the exact HTTPS webhook URL that your phone provider should call, for example https://your.domain/voice/webhook.
onboarding.conversationAutomationWebhookPrivateAcce: Tailscale is not ready on this Mac yet. Finish that setup first, then come back here.
onboarding.conversationAutomationWebhookAdminSubtit: Your tailnet still has Funnel disabled. Open the admin page to enable it, or switch to a manual public webhook URL instead.
onboarding.conversationAutomationSttSubtitle: Choose the engine Maumau should use while a phone call is live. Both options need their own API key.
onboarding.conversationAutomationDeepgramAPIKeySubt: Open the Deepgram console, create or copy a project API key, then paste it here.
onboarding.conversationAutomationOpenAIAPIKeySubtit: Open the OpenAI API keys page, create a key for the Realtime API, then paste it here.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTtsSubtitle: Maumau uses ElevenLabs with eleven_multilingual_v2 for call replies. Add the API key here, and optionally override the default voice.
onboarding.conversationAutomationElevenLabsAPIKeySu: Open ElevenLabs API authentication docs or your workspace settings, then paste the key here.
onboarding.conversationAutomationElevenLabsVoiceIDS: Leave this blank to use Maumau’s multilingual default voice, or paste a voice ID from the ElevenLabs voice library.
onboarding.conversationAutomationReadySubtitle: This voice-call setup now has the required configuration for the mode you chose.
onboarding.conversationAutomationBeforeFinishTitle: Finish is blocked until these are added
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationFromNumb: Add the phone number Maumau should call from.
Use E.164 format for the phone number, for example +628123456789.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationVapiAPIK: Add the Vapi API key, then connect to load assistants and phone numbers.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationVapiPhon: Choose one imported Twilio phone number from Vapi.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationVapiBrid: Finish Private Access first so Maumau has a public bridge URL for Vapi.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationTelnyxCo: Add the Telnyx Call Control connection or application ID.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationTelnyxPu: Add the Telnyx public key for webhook verification.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationTailscal: Install Tailscale on this Mac or switch to a manual public webhook URL.
Install Tailscale on this Mac for Auto bridge, or switch the Vapi bridge to Manual public URL.
Sign in to Tailscale on this Mac or switch to a manual public webhook URL.
Sign in to Tailscale on this Mac for Auto bridge, or switch the Vapi bridge to Manual public URL.
Enable Tailscale Funnel for this tailnet or switch to a manual public webhook URL.
Enable Tailscale Funnel for this tailnet for Auto bridge, or switch the Vapi bridge to Manual public URL.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationVapiManu: Add the public HTTPS URL that Vapi should use for the Maumau bridge.
Use a valid HTTPS bridge URL, for example https://your.domain/plugins/voice-call/vapi.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationPublicWe: Add the public HTTPS webhook URL that your phone provider should call.
Use a valid HTTPS webhook URL, for example https://your.domain/voice/webhook.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationDeepgram: Add the Deepgram API key or switch the speech engine to OpenAI Realtime.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationOpenAIAP: Add the OpenAI API key or switch the speech engine to Deepgram Nova-3.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationElevenLa: Add the ElevenLabs API key for spoken replies.
onboarding.conversationAutomationValidationListHead: Add the missing items below, then Finish will save a working voice-call config.
onboarding.conversationAutomationTailscaleUnavailab: Tailscale Funnel is not ready yet
onboarding.conversationAutomationTailscaleReadyTitl: Use this callback URL in your phone provider
onboarding.skillsIntro: This is the short version of the core tools Maumau already comes with on this Mac. On first-time local setup, Maumau also installs nano-pdf, OpenAI Whisper, and summarize automatically when they are missing, while bundled setup guides like Clawd Cursor help you turn on extra capabilities later.
onboarding.memoryTitle: Long-term memory, when you want it
onboarding.memorySubtitle: Maumau keeps long-term memory private for each user while also sharing approved context with the groups they belong to. Open Users later for details on people, groups, and sharing.
onboarding.openFullSkillsSubtitle: See everything that is available, including the bundled Clawd Cursor setup guide, Cursor-compatible bundles, and extra tools you can turn on or off later.
onboarding.checkingIncludedSkillsTitle: Checking which included skills are available here…
onboarding.readyHeadline: Maumau now has a home, a brain, and a place people can reach it.
onboarding.readyBody: You can keep things simple for now and fine-tune the rest later in Settings.
onboarding.managedBrowserSignInTitle: Sign in once to Maumau's browser
onboarding.managedBrowserSignInSubtitle: This opens Maumau's separate browser profile on this Mac. Sign in there to any sites you want browser automation to reuse later. You can close it afterward.
onboarding.managedBrowserSignInOpenedStatus: Maumau's browser profile is open. Sign in there once, and Maumau can reopen that same profile later.
onboarding.managedBrowserSignInFailedStatusPrefix: Couldn’t open Maumau's browser profile yet.
onboarding.configureLaterSubtitle: Pick Local or Remote in Settings → General whenever you’re ready.
onboarding.menuBarPanelSubtitle: Click the Maumau menu bar icon for quick chat and status.
onboarding.voiceWakeSubtitle: Enable Voice Wake in Settings for hands-free commands with a live transcript overlay.
onboarding.panelCanvasSubtitle: Open the menu bar panel for quick chat; the agent can show previews and richer visuals in Canvas.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiAutoBridgeSubt: Maumau will publish {bridgeURL} through Tailscale Funnel so Vapi can reach the live tool-calls bridge without changing your normal private-access path.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiBridgeSubtitle: Vapi will call {bridgeURL} so Maumau can generate each spoken reply with tools and memory.
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiRefreshReady: Connected to Vapi. Found {assistantCount} assistant(s) and {phoneNumberCount} phone number(s).
onboarding.conversationAutomationVapiRefreshFailed: Could not load your Vapi assistants or phone numbers. {detail}

welcomeIntro.remote: Setup is simpler than it looks: set up the Gateway, then pick a Channel for messages.
welcomeIntro.local: Setup is simpler than it looks: choose the brain, pick a Channel, then turn on any Mac access or extras you want.

localSetupPreparationTitle.busy: Getting Maumau’s home ready before the brain step starts…
localSetupPreparationTitle.idle: This Mac still needs a little setup first

localSetupPreparationMessage.installingCLI: Maumau is installing the helper pieces it needs on this Mac.
localSetupPreparationMessage.checkingLocalGateway: Maumau is checking whether this Mac already has what it needs.
localSetupPreparationMessage.finishLocal: Finish getting this Mac ready first. Once that is done, the brain setup continues automatically.

conversationAutomationWebhookTailscaleSu.expectedURL: Maumau will publish {expectedURL} and you should use that same URL in your phone provider's webhook setting.
conversationAutomationWebhookTailscaleSu.default: Maumau will publish /voice/webhook over Tailscale Funnel and you should use that same URL in your phone provider's webhook setting.

home.title: Gateway
home.explainerBody: Gateway means Maumau's home. It keeps its tools here and does its work from here.

brain.title: Brain
brain.explainerBody: Brain means the AI service. You are choosing what does the thinking and writing.

chat.title: Channel
chat.explainerBody: Channel means where people can reach Maumau. Think of it like giving it a phone line or inbox.

access.title: Private access
access.explainerBody: This gives Maumau's home a private driveway. It lets your phone, laptop, or browser reach Maumau privately without putting it on the public internet.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.headerSubtitle: What Maumau can do on this Mac
permissions.explainerBody: This is where you decide what Maumau can do on this Mac, like work with apps or see the screen.

automation.title: Voice
automation.explainerBody: This optional step finishes the provider keys and public callback route that the built-in voice-call plugin needs before real phone calls can work.

tools.title: Tools
tools.explainerBody: This is a quick look at the main tools Maumau already has, so you know what comes with it.

includedToolHighlights.title: Files and folders
includedToolHighlights.subtitle: Read, organize, and change things on this Mac when you allow it.

includedToolHighlights.title: Apps and screen context
includedToolHighlights.subtitle: Work with Mac apps and screenshots when the matching permissions are on.

includedToolHighlights.title: Browser control
includedToolHighlights.subtitle: Open websites, follow links, and work through everyday web tasks in a browser.

includedToolHighlights.title: Commands
includedToolHighlights.subtitle: Run Terminal commands when you approve them or allow them.

includedToolHighlights.title: Messages and connected services
includedToolHighlights.subtitle: Reply in the Channel you picked and use any extra services you connect later.

includedHelperHighlights.title: Clawd Cursor
includedHelperHighlights.subtitle: Fresh local setup installs the upstream clawdcursor helper for native desktop control across apps, then Maumau keeps checking readiness and permissions truthfully.

includedHelperHighlights.title: Maumau Guardrails
includedHelperHighlights.subtitle: Keeps prompts, tool calls, and outgoing replies inside your policy once you connect a guardrails sidecar.

includedHelperHighlights.title: Lobster workflows
includedHelperHighlights.subtitle: Automates repeatable, multi-step tasks with resumable approvals instead of making the agent improvise every step.

includedHelperHighlights.title: Structured AI tasks
includedHelperHighlights.subtitle: Uses LLM Task for clean JSON output, which helps with forms, extraction, handoffs, and workflow steps.

messages.whatsappConnected: WhatsApp is ready. Maumau is already using the recommended defaults, and you can change advanced routing or access rules later in full Settings → Channels.
messages.whatsappDisconnected: If you want to change approved numbers, routing, or other advanced WhatsApp behavior later, use full Settings → Channels. Maumau keeps the recommended defaults unless you change them.
messages.connectedGeneric: {channelTitle} is already connected. Maumau is using the recommended defaults, and you can review or override them later in full Settings → Channels.
messages.disconnectedGeneric: Onboarding is only showing the key setup details for {channelTitle}. When you are ready, open full Settings → Channels to paste the token or finish the account/device connection. Maumau will use the recommended defaults automatically for the rest.

setupMode.title: Simple or custom
setupMode.bodyText: This is Maumau asking how much of the setup work you want it to handle for you.

existingSetup.title: Keep or reset
existingSetup.bodyText: Maumau found an older home setup and wants to know whether to reuse it or start fresh.

pickBrain.title: Pick the brain
pickBrain.bodyText: You are choosing which AI service or model does the thinking for Maumau.

liveSearch.title: Add live search
liveSearch.bodyText: This optional step lets Maumau look up current information on the web when it needs it.

connectBrain.title: Connect the brain
connectBrain.bodyText: This is the sign-in step so Maumau can actually talk to the AI service you picked.

workspace.title: Pick Maumau’s room
workspace.bodyText: This is the folder where Maumau keeps notes, reads instructions, and makes files.

preparingSetup.title: A quick setup moment
preparingSetup.bodyText: Maumau is just getting the next brain setup step ready for you.

Type a message below to start.

canvasHome.offlineTitle: Your phone stays quiet until it is needed
canvasHome.offlineSubtitle: Pair this device to your gateway to wake it only for real work, keep a live agent overview handy, and avoid battery-draining background loops.
canvasHome.offlineFooter: When connected, the gateway can wake the phone with a silent push instead of holding an always-on session.
canvasHome.emptyConnected: Your gateway is online. Agents will appear here as soon as the current scope reports them.
canvasHome.emptyOffline: Connect this phone to your gateway and the live agent overview will appear here.
canvasHome.connectedSubtitle: This phone stays dormant until the gateway needs it, then wakes, syncs, and goes back to sleep.
canvasHome.connectedFooter: The overview refreshes on reconnect and when the app returns to foreground.
canvasHome.connectingSubtitle: The gateway session is coming back online. Agent shortcuts should settle automatically in a moment.
canvasHome.connectingFooter: If the gateway is reachable, reconnect should complete without intervention.
